steps.reveal: Facilis = easy (nom. sg., predicate adjective with 'descensus')

hints.genitive: Genitive singular is also 'facilis', but here it is nominative, modifying the subject 'descensus'
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'facili' — 'facilis' is nominative here
hints.accusative: Accusative masc. sg. would be 'facilem' — 'facilis' is nominative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'facilis' ends in -is (3rd declension adjective)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'facilis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'facilis' ends in -is

hints.genitive: Genitive singular would also be 'descensus' (4th decl.), but here it is nominative subject
hints.accusative: Accusative plural would be 'descensus', but here it is nominative singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'descensus' has gen. in -ūs (4th declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension -us nouns have genitive in -i — 'descensus' has gen. in -ūs
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'descensus' has gen. in -ūs

steps.reveal: Averno = to/toward Avernus (dat. sg., dative of direction)

hints.ablative: While 'Averno' could be ablative, here it is dative — 'the descent to Avernus is easy'
hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Avernus' — 'Averno' is dative singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'Averni' ends in -i (2nd declension)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'Averni' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'Averni' ends in -i

steps.prompt: There is an implied verb in this sentence. What is it?
steps.reveal: est (is) is implied: 'The descent to Avernus is easy'

hints.manet: 'Manet' means 'remains' — the implied verb is simply 'est' (is)

translation: Easy is the descent to Avernus
